metadata.description: Acme Corp microservices architecture and team ownership

services.name: api-gateway
services.description: Main entry point for all client requests. Handles routing, authentication, rate limiting, and request transformation.

services.name: auth-service
services.description: Handles user authentication, JWT token generation, OAuth2 flows, and session management.

services.name: user-service
services.description: Manages user profiles, preferences, and account data. Provides user CRUD operations.

services.name: payments-service
services.description: Processes payments via Stripe and PayPal. Handles payment intents, refunds, and transaction history.

services.name: order-service
services.description: Manages order lifecycle: creation, updates, fulfillment, and cancellation. Orchestrates payments and inventory.

services.name: inventory-service
services.description: Tracks product inventory levels, handles stock reservations, and manages warehouse data.

services.name: product-service
services.description: Manages product catalog, categories, pricing, and product search indexing.

services.name: search-service
services.description: Provides full-text search across products, orders, and users. Powered by Elasticsearch.

services.name: notification-service
services.description: Handles email, SMS, and push notifications. Processes notification queue and manages templates.

services.name: analytics-service
services.description: Collects and processes business metrics, user behavior data, and generates reports.

teams.name: platform-team
teams.description: Responsible for core infrastructure, authentication, and API gateway.

teams.name: commerce-team
teams.description: Handles all commerce-related services: payments, orders, inventory, and products.

teams.name: growth-team
teams.description: Focuses on user acquisition, retention, notifications, and user experience.

teams.name: data-team
teams.description: Manages search, analytics, data pipelines, and reporting infrastructure.

sections.title: Common Issues
sections.content:
1. High latency: Check upstream service health, review rate limiting config
2. 502/503 errors: Verify backend services are running, check network connectivity
3. Rate limit errors: Review rate limit configuration in Redis

sections.title: Restart Procedure
sections.content:
kubectl rollout restart deployment/api-gateway -n production
Monitor with: kubectl get pods -n production -l app=api-gateway -w

sections.title: Escalation
sections.content:
Contact Platform Team on-call: #platform-team Slack channel
PagerDuty: platform-critical service

sections.title: Connection Pool Issues
sections.content:
1. Check current pool size: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE datname = 'payments_db'
2. Increase pool size in config: DB_POOL_SIZE=100
3. Restart service: kubectl rollout restart deployment/payments-service

sections.title: Stripe API Failures
sections.content:
1. Check Stripe status: https://status.stripe.com
2. If Stripe is down, circuit breaker should auto-activate
3. Manual fallback: Set PAYMENT_PROVIDER=paypal in config
4. Monitor: Grafana dashboard 'Payments - Provider Health'

sections.title: Transaction Failures
sections.content:
1. Check payment logs: kubectl logs -l app=payments-service | grep ERROR
2. Verify database connectivity
3. Check Stripe webhook delivery status
4. Review failed transactions in payments table

sections.title: Escalation
sections.content:
Contact Commerce Team on-call: #commerce-team Slack channel
PagerDuty: commerce-critical service
For Stripe issues: Contact Stripe support via dashboard

sections.title: Memory Issues
sections.content:
1. Check memory usage: kubectl top pod -l app=search-service
2. If >80%, increase memory limit in deployment
3. Check for memory leaks: Review recent deployments
4. Restart if necessary: kubectl rollout restart deployment/search-service

sections.title: Elasticsearch Issues
sections.content:
1. Check ES cluster health: curl -s http://elasticsearch:9200/_cluster/health
2. Check index status: curl -s http://elasticsearch:9200/_cat/indices
3. If red status, check shard allocation
4. Rebuild index if corrupted: POST /_reindex

sections.title: Slow Queries
sections.content:
1. Check slow query log: GET /_search slowlog
2. Optimize query with filters instead of queries where possible
3. Add pagination to large result sets
4. Consider caching frequent queries in Redis

sections.title: Escalation
sections.content:
Contact Data Team on-call: #data-team Slack channel
PagerDuty: data-standard service

sections.title: Database Deadlocks
sections.content:
1. Check for deadlocks: SELECT * FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE wait_event_type = 'Lock'
2. Kill blocking queries: SELECT pg_terminate_backend(pid) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE ...
3. Review transaction isolation levels
4. Ensure proper row-level locking with SELECT FOR UPDATE

sections.title: Order Processing Delays
sections.content:
1. Check order queue depth: Redis LLEN order_queue
2. Verify payment-service and inventory-service health
3. Check for stuck orders: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'processing' AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour'
4. Manually retry stuck orders if needed

sections.title: Escalation
sections.content:
Contact Commerce Team on-call: #commerce-team Slack channel
PagerDuty: commerce-critical service
